_meta.source: envlib-defined (ported from tethys for migration continuity); no upstream refresh

entries.name: derivation
entries.description: A method for creating results by simple calculations from field activities, sample analyses, or sensor recordings.

entries.name: estimation
entries.description: A method for creating results by rough approximation or professional judgement. Does not use an analytical or numerical model.

entries.name: field_activity
entries.description: A method for creating results by performing an activity in the field at or on a sampling feature. Includes manually-operated instruments (e.g., handheld probes, manual gauging, field surveys) - anything requiring a human at the measurement location.

entries.name: simulation
entries.description: A method for creating results by running an analytical or numerical model. Generally more complex and/or more uncertain than derivations. Used for climate model runs, reanalyses (e.g., ERA5), and other model outputs.

entries.name: sample_analysis
entries.description: A method for ex situ analysis of a sample using an instrument, typically in a laboratory, for the purpose of measuring properties of a sample.

entries.name: sensor_recording
entries.description: A method for creating results by independent, automated sensor measurements without direct human interaction at the sensor during the measurement. Includes in-situ data loggers and remote sensing.

entries.name: forecast
entries.description: A type of simulation to predict the future. Kept distinct from simulation so consumers can filter forecasts from historical model runs.
